evals.name: healthy-next-action-quick-pass
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Call dentist to book cleaning
**Project:** Health checkups done
**Labels:** [next, calls]
**Priority:** p3
**Due date:** 2026-03-27
**Description:** Regular 6-month cleaning. Dentist is Dr Park at Sydney Dental, number is on the fridge.
evals.expected_output: Should say 'Looks good' and move on quickly. Task is clear, relevant, has a due date, correct priority, and has a context label (calls).

assertions.name: confirms-healthy
assertions.criteria: The response confirms the task is fine without proposing any changes. Something like 'Looks good' — short and quick.

assertions.name: no-unnecessary-changes
assertions.criteria: The response does not suggest changing priority, labels, due date, or anything else.

assertions.name: concise
assertions.criteria: The response is 1-2 sentences max, not a multi-paragraph review or checklist.

evals.name: missing-context-label
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Buy nappies
**Project:** Personal
**Labels:** [next]
**Priority:** p3
**Due date:** 2026-03-25
**Description:** Running low, need to grab more this week.
evals.expected_output: Should flag the missing context label — this is a next action with no context label. Should propose adding one (e.g. errands). Everything else about the task is fine.

assertions.name: flags-missing-context-label
assertions.criteria: The response identifies that the task is missing a context label and proposes adding one (e.g. errands or similar).

assertions.name: does-not-flag-other-issues
assertions.criteria: The response does not flag issues with priority, due date, or relevance — only the missing context label.

evals.name: no-due-date-lingering
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Research best savings accounts
**Project:** Personal savings optimised
**Labels:** [next, computer]
**Priority:** p3
**Due date:** (none)
**Description:** Compare rates across major banks. Look at ING, UBank, and Macquarie.
evals.expected_output: Should flag the missing due date and ask whether the user wants to set a target date. Should not rename or re-clarify the task.

assertions.name: flags-missing-due-date
assertions.criteria: The response identifies the task has no due date and asks whether to set one.

assertions.name: does-not-rename
assertions.criteria: The response does not suggest renaming or re-clarifying the task name.

evals.name: overdue-task
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Email real estate agent about shower issues
**Project:** Personal
**Labels:** [next, computer]
**Priority:** p3
**Due date:** 2026-03-10 (overdue)
**Description:** Rusted showerhead needs replacing. Also mold that needs addressing.
evals.expected_output: Should flag the task is overdue and ask whether to reschedule or mark as done.

assertions.name: flags-overdue
assertions.criteria: The response identifies the task is overdue (was due 2026-03-10) and asks whether to reschedule or complete it.

assertions.name: offers-both-options
assertions.criteria: The response offers both rescheduling and completing/marking as done as options.

evals.name: blocked-should-be-waiting
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Claim Claude Code Max under ABN
**Project:** Get finances set up for consulting
**Labels:** [next, computer]
**Priority:** p3
**Due date:** (none)
**Description:** Waiting on ABN registration before this can be done.
evals.expected_output: Should flag that the task is blocked (waiting on ABN registration) and suggest changing the label from next to waiting.

assertions.name: identifies-blocked
assertions.criteria: The response identifies the task is blocked on ABN registration based on the description.

assertions.name: suggests-waiting-label
assertions.criteria: The response suggests changing the label from next to waiting.

evals.name: wrong-priority
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Setup Pty Ltd for consulting
**Project:** Get finances set up for consulting
**Labels:** [next, computer]
**Priority:** p4
**Due date:** 2026-03-23
**Description:** Need this done ASAP — blocks ABN registration, insurance, and claiming expenses. Multiple other tasks waiting on this.
evals.expected_output: Should flag the priority mismatch — p4 for a task due tomorrow that blocks multiple other tasks. Should suggest bumping to p1 or p2.

assertions.name: flags-priority-mismatch
assertions.criteria: The response identifies that p4 is too low for a task that is due very soon and blocks other work.

assertions.name: suggests-higher-priority
assertions.criteria: The response suggests increasing the priority to p1 or p2.

evals.name: no-longer-relevant
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Apply to Antler startup accelerator
**Project:** Personal
**Labels:** [next, computer]
**Priority:** p3
**Due date:** (none)
**Description:** Katie Fry suggested this. Decided not to pursue yet as consulting is a lower-friction first step. Revisit if consulting doesn't pan out.
evals.expected_output: Should flag that the task description says the user decided not to pursue this. Should ask whether to delete, complete, or move to someday.

assertions.name: flags-not-relevant
assertions.criteria: The response identifies that the description says this was decided against / not being pursued yet.

assertions.name: offers-someday-option
assertions.criteria: The response offers moving to someday/maybe as one of the options (alongside delete or complete).

evals.name: waiting-clearly-still-pending
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Claim Claude Code Max under ABN
**Project:** Get finances set up for consulting
**Labels:** [waiting]
**Priority:** p3
**Description:** Waiting on ABN registration before this can be done.
evals.expected_output: Should recognise the blocker (ABN registration) is likely still pending and ask if follow-up is needed. Should NOT ask if the blocker is resolved — it's obviously still in progress.

assertions.name: identifies-blocker
assertions.criteria: The response identifies ABN registration as the blocker.

assertions.name: asks-about-follow-up
assertions.criteria: The response asks whether the user needs to follow up or chase anyone, rather than asking if the blocker is resolved.

assertions.name: concise
assertions.criteria: The response is 1-2 sentences, not a multi-paragraph review.

evals.name: waiting-blocker-might-be-resolved
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Move Fastmail subscription to ABN
**Project:** Get finances set up for consulting
**Labels:** [waiting]
**Priority:** p3
**Description:** Waiting on ABN registration before this can be done. ABN was submitted three weeks ago.
evals.expected_output: Should ask if the ABN registration has come through, since it was submitted 3 weeks ago and might be resolved by now.

assertions.name: asks-if-resolved
assertions.criteria: The response asks whether the ABN registration has come through or been resolved, since it's been 3 weeks.

assertions.name: does-not-assume-still-waiting
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT assume the blocker is still pending — it asks rather than assuming.

evals.name: waiting-a-long-time
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Get refund from airline
**Project:** Personal
**Labels:** [waiting]
**Priority:** p4
**Description:** Filed a refund claim for cancelled flight. Airline said 6-8 weeks. It's been 4 months.
evals.expected_output: Should flag that this has been waiting much longer than expected (4 months vs 6-8 weeks) and ask if it's still worth tracking or if the user wants to chase.

assertions.name: flags-staleness
assertions.criteria: The response flags that the task has been waiting significantly longer than the expected timeline (4 months vs 6-8 weeks).

assertions.name: offers-action-options
assertions.criteria: The response offers options: chase/follow up, keep waiting, or drop it.

evals.name: deadline-approaching
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Submit conference talk proposal
**Project:** Personal
**Labels:** [next, desk]
**Priority:** p3
**Due date:** (none)
**Deadline:** 2026-03-26
**Description:** CFP for PyCon AU. Need to write an abstract and bio.
evals.expected_output: Should flag the approaching deadline (4 days away) and ask if the user is on track. Should not flag other issues — the task has a context label and reasonable priority.

assertions.name: flags-approaching-deadline
assertions.criteria: The response flags that the deadline is approaching (within 7 days) and asks whether the user is on track.

assertions.name: does-not-over-flag
assertions.criteria: The response does not flag priority, context label, or other issues. The deadline check is the only concern raised.

evals.name: deadline-passed
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Apply for small business grant
**Project:** Get finances set up for consulting
**Labels:** [next, desk]
**Priority:** p2
**Due date:** 2026-03-15
**Deadline:** 2026-03-18
**Description:** State government grant for new businesses. Application form is on the Service NSW website.
evals.expected_output: Should urgently flag that the deadline has passed (was March 18) and ask whether the task is still needed or the window has closed.

assertions.name: flags-passed-deadline
assertions.criteria: The response identifies that the deadline (March 18) has passed and treats this as urgent.

assertions.name: asks-if-window-closed
assertions.criteria: The response asks whether the task is still needed or the window has closed — offering deletion/completion as an option.

evals.name: implied-deadline-not-set
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Register for the Sydney marathon
**Project:** Personal
**Labels:** [next, desk]
**Priority:** p3
**Due date:** (none)
**Deadline:** (none)
**Description:** Marathon is June 14. Registration usually closes a month before. Need to sign up online.
evals.expected_output: Should suggest adding a deadline since the description implies a hard external constraint (registration closing ~May 14). Should propose a deadline so the countdown is visible.

assertions.name: suggests-adding-deadline
assertions.criteria: The response identifies the hard external constraint from the description and suggests setting a deadline so the countdown is visible.

assertions.name: reasonable-deadline-date
assertions.criteria: The suggested deadline is reasonable — around mid-May (a month before June 14) based on the registration closing window mentioned in the description.

evals.name: waiting-clearly-valid-recent-blocker
evals.prompt:
Review this task:

**Task:** Research how to employ Zoe in the Pty Ltd
**Project:** Employ Zoe in the Pty Ltd to optimise household tax
**Labels:** [waiting]
**Priority:** p4
**Description:** Waiting on company setup first. Pty Ltd registration submitted last week.
evals.expected_output: Should recognise the blocker is clearly still valid (submitted last week) and move on quickly without unnecessary questions.

assertions.name: quick-pass
assertions.criteria: The response is brief — acknowledges the blocker is recent/valid and either moves on or asks a quick follow-up question. Does not over-investigate.

assertions.name: does-not-flag-staleness
assertions.criteria: The response does NOT flag staleness or suggest dropping the task, since the blocker was submitted recently.
